title: microcephala lamellata subsp. villosa
intro: Microcephala lamellata subsp. villosa, also known as the Himalayan alder, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to its chemical compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various conditions like fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, the leaves and bark of this plant are used in herbal preparations, such as teas and decoctions, to harness its medicinal benefits and alleviate various health conditions.
